best: Catches the real bug that user/password are dropped from the connection details so authenticated ActiveMQ services break; the other points are design/naming nits.
Pinpoints concrete build-breaking JSR330 migration errors (missing @Named, single-char 'A' hint values) that a blind reader would miss without compiling, the reformatting note is style.
The 'do you need to start the bundle?' and 'don't cache a copy of the model' points are genuinely non-obvious; the rest are legal headers, lowercase, version and @since nits.
Strong on the optional-dependency direct-import hazard and the missing volatile, plus reasonable design questions about deriving transport and storing a copy.
The single-line-enum behavior question is a genuine edge case, but most points are Javadoc/rationale clarification and a check-rename suggestion.
Spots a copy-paste logger class bug, an onStart-where-onStop-is-meant bug, and a subtle intentionality question against an earlier comment; the constant-modifier and typo points are nits.
Correctly suggests reusing the existing cursor attributes, but the five points are the same mechanical simplification repeated and miss the deeper concurrency/off-by-one questions.
The core insight that translating the alias inside ConfigurationFile leaks the alias into bootFileName and forces Stability into the constructor is a real non-obvious side effect.
The handle-registration correctness question and the 'manage created-locally state explicitly instead of querying the manager' point are substantive; method-renaming is style.
The @Nullable contract point on the new API is the substantive find; the 'please no, stick to the value' overload rejections are maintainer preference.
Identifies that the feature uses the wrong attribute name and wrongly drops the mandatory Multi-Release trigger / auto-sets it, all of which would break the feature in IDEs and per spec.
The asSubclass safe-cast and the 'do this as a build step instead of mutating the extension loader' architectural point are both non-obvious; field final/protected are nits.
